item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Staff Post
item.content:encoded: I'm not a fan of Heimdall at all— it seems like any SCP that even remotely involves extraterrestrials can have it shoehorned in. The link's been removed.

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Not the biggest fan of this edit. In my headcanon, this thing ain't alien, which kind of negates the Heimdall link.

If anything, I'd support a link to SCP-1000, simply because of the connections already pointed out in this thread.

item.title: Wikiwalk Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- Hide

In the sections where they believe/pretend the reptiles are aliens, it would make sense to mention the Foundation's anti alien wing, so:

Once approximately 20% global awareness of SCP-2013 has been reached, the Hereford Protocol is to be carried out.

to

Once approximately 20% global awareness of SCP-2013 has been reached, the Hereford Protocol is to be carried out by Project Heimdall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually my favorite SCP-2000 entry. Ever better then SCP-2000 in my opinion. Shocked it doesn't have more upvotes.

+1
This was probably one of the more mediocre efforts in the 2000 contest. To see it finally hit +100 is amazing. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh man, that "extra" just made me crack up completely.

And what about this?

O.o

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The final "twist" reminded me of SCP-920-EX, but on a much larger scale. Very nicely written, and definitely one of the more paranoia inspiring skips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… David Icke is right?

+ Also, 
- 

this guy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can safely say that this was probably one of the most controversial entries of the contest. Maybe. Possibly. Probably not.

Anyway. On to SCP-3000?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a perfect mirror to 1000's bigfoot re-write, a reptiloid re-write.
Unfortunately…. I don't think 1000 should be bigfoot. It's not nearly momentous or grand enough.
So I don't think this should get to be its' twin of 2000… but it surely will attain a place of honour in the SCP catalogue… just not that particular place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why on earth would the foundation use humanoid reptiles in people suits to infiltrate the government? When they could, well I dont know, use actual trained reliable human agent.

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Neutral vote does not mean that people don't care about your article. The exact opposite. When people go "meh, I didn't give a shit about any of this", then they downvote. Here people are neutral voting because they like the article, but they don't want it to be a SCP-2000.

There isn't any reasonable chance that you are winning the contest. That being said, you are already a better person than me, because I didn't even participate in the contest. I was afraid of criticism that much.

Also I enjoyed your article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pros: Reptiloids, chemtrails, false flag operations, shadow agencies manipulating governments, double-bluffs and pretty much every other conspiracy theory trope all in one well-written, in-universe-plausible package. I particularly love the final implication about O5-2. The irony of the Foundation getting hoist on their own petard is just great.

Cons: Too similar to 1000 in concept, Samothrace in presentation, and much, much too similar to the Slavine (of Raxacoricofallapatorius via Dr. Who) in detail.

I feel like I'd up-vote if presented as a tale; but as a mainlister, particularly as a contender for a prestige spot, my net feeling is negative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1000 made Bigfoot into a believable SCP. This potential 2000 makes the Reptiloid conspiracy into one.

This is exactly why I wanted to like this. It's just so neat.

However, this is really "meh" and the supplement seesaws between really awesome & really bad. (I go into more detail on that page's comments.)

It's well-written, but that's not enough. Downvoted, but with a sadface.

Although, for the record:

Telling an author that your reaction to their work is a neutral one is the largest insult you can give them, worse than telling them that you think it sucked.

I don't agree with this. I commonly neutral-vote things when I can't decide if I like them or not — usually I like one element, dislike another, and can't decide how it balances out in my head. So I wait to see if I do gain feelings for something afterwards. (And this often does happen — I may develop extremely positive or extremely negative reactions to that article after it's had a chance to 'sit' for longer in my head.)

EDIT: Also, I couldn't say in advance how I'd vote if the supplement were fixed. It depends on how the two work together — they are inextricably link; while I happened to have separate reactions to each as if they were separate pieces, it's entirely possible this piece would work just fine for me unchanged if the supplement was fixed. It depends.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the idea of another conspiracy theory getting made into Foundation canon, so KUDOS, good sir!
1000 = Bigfoot
2000 = Reptiloids
3000 = ?????

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I do agree with thism, but one can understand that the early days Foundation really needed to the get the resources by any means neccessary. The stuff they do "nowadays" is based on already established and well-rooted organisation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmm… no 682. Yep, it's the lizardmen conspiracy! This legitimately made me go from "wtf" to "lol", if you'd like. Even the skin suits! And they inhabit the Insurgency; come on man, no Serpent's hand?

Foundation Agents will be dispatched to your location if this window is not closed.

Ack

Click here to return to SCP-2000

Clever girl. And boy, here we go. I sure hoped this wouldn't be all.

experimental compound that's long since been lost to history. It was made up of mercury, iridium, radium, and a few other elements that science didn't yet know about.

Sounds a liiitle farfetched to me they had access to the compound and SUDDENLY it worked. Maybe using another 500-esque SCP they already had would work better? And .. even the masks? Isn't this all too convenient, too many coincidences?

Czechoslovakia

+ ^_^
- hehe

Commenter is Czech

You know, when I read about the lizardmen really running the show, the article got from silly to creepy fast. You even managed to include the chemtrails. And even O5-2 is…
Holy crap. And a cherry on top, dat ending.

You know, my only issue with this was that it didn't go with the mainstream TV trope of sci-fi, as in space and aliens, too much. Even the eggs could have been from Atlantis AFAIK. But somehow, the story and the take on the whole conspiracy theory was so well adapted and fleshed out, that I am going to overlook that fact and reward you with a +1. Heck, you even managed to somehow tangle in Czechoslovakia and make be goosebump a little. Gotta love those fourth wall SCPs.

+1

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I shouldn't worry too much about neutral votes - I think a lot of them are coming because of the contest. People neutral vote when they like it, but not for 2000. I do completely understand the pain of feeling ignored, though.

For what it's worth (as the reset button is almost certainly going to win), I really liked this one. I would have been happy to see it as 2000. It's a fun myth, taken on a nice spin. I'm a big fan of ambiguity, and this one handles it particularly well. I like that I can keep my own interpretation, which is that the final supplement document is just written by someone infected with the meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this stands firm in the running for SCP-2000, well done! There was one thing that confused me however, the leap from "keter" to "safe". It's seem like a very rare instinct for that to occur, perhaps "euclid" to "safe" would fit better. Though your SCP is dangerous to man "keter" gives the idea of hatred of all humanity, I didn't feel that in this. With that said I think this is really good, so you've earned my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it and think it is well-done. But I'm neutral voting for the fact that it doesn't fit my Foundation Head Canon, and certainly not for slot 2000. Although I also hate SCP-1000, so there's that.

I would upvote this as a tale. But not as a mainlist.

EDIT: Just read your rant. As I said, I would upvote it as a tale, and I feel really bad downvoting something that I like just because this isn't the proper venue for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You forgot about the caveat that it has to be the mother doing it, willfully, and that it takes hours for the baby to die.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Foundation wouldn't do that. Seriously. Ever.

Yes, you're right. It wouldn't. But you know what it would do?

  • Sacrifice babies to prevent natural disasters that may or may not actually be linked to a statue of a demon
  • Incinerate a sapient organism that lives in books because it makes things look bad and has a chance of causing sickness
  • Recruit a teenaged girl who can use telekinesis through photographs onto a task force, along with an immortal psychopath who can generate weapons out of thin air
  • Keep a researcher on staff even after he does all of this shit
  • Recklessly experiment with a vending machine that produces food not fit for human consumption on a regular basis, and then allow researchers to eat from it
  • Using a coffee machine that can generate any liquid to produce molten gold for the sake of profit
  • Using a young child to cure diseases that then manifest in himself, and then mentally retarding him to the point that he's mentally four years old

Yes, I can see how you would think that using supernatural techniques to gain profit from countries reluctant to give it would be far-fetched. After all, the Foundation never abuses what it is given, does it?

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, Ihpkmn, but fair is fair. If you want people to downvote your skip, you'll need to earn it the old-fashioned way through bad writing.

(Seriously, it's fine. Stop worrying about it.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tone: The Foundation wouldn't do that. Seriously. Ever. (Sorry, I didn't mean Clinical Tone. I meant the tone of the piece. Maybe "Mood" would have been a better word for me to use.)

Containment Procedures: SCP-2000 is currently Uncontained. The reason it is uncontained is because of actions taken by the Foundation while it was successfully conatined. If proper Containment Procedures had been utilized in the first place, this would never have occurred.

I can see now that I used these terms too liberally in my first post, and I apologize for that.

(Sorry I didn't see your post in the Forums, I was probably already polishing mine up for final submission. The good news is, the contest rules say you can make changes up until the end of the submission phase, so there's still time! Your rating seems fine at the moment, so I wouldn't worry about it too much unless you have a nice tidy fix in mind. Good luck!)

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Ihpkmn, I've worked for days on tales that don't get 20 votes. You're sitting at a very respectable score in a crowded field and I think you need to manage your expectations. Don't dismiss the upvotes you got just because of downvotes you didn't get. And don't demand a response that you couldn't generate in the first place with your writing.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
The resounding reaction to this article, going by the comments, has been "meh". Either the article is weak and the supplement is strong, the supplement is weak and the article is strong, they both suck but they're well written… either way, it's not good enough for an upvote, but it's not good enough for a downvote either. That's what you're conveying to me. That it just isn't good enough either way.

So, I have a request for you: if you think it's "meh", please, downvote it. This may seem like I'm asking for pity-upvotes, but I'm not. I want anyone who think it's "meh" to downvote it because I want to know I got some kind of a reaction out of my audience other than a "meh", even if that reaction is a negative one.

Telling an author that your reaction to their work is a neutral one is the largest insult you can give them, worse than telling them that you think it sucked. So, telling me that you're neutral-voting this article for one reason or another, one that I worked on for days in preparation for this contest, one that I submitted the instant the floodgates opened because I was so excited to be a part of this, hurts. A negative reaction is still a reaction, and that's what I want to get out of my audience: a quantifiable reaction in the form of votes.

It's not my intent to win this contest. I just want to do relatively well in it, maybe make fourth place, at best. I just want to have something that I'm proud up on the 2000 series when this contest ends, even if it doesn't win. And now, I'm having trouble being proud of this work because so many people think it's not worth voting on.

Sorry for any drama this post may cause. I just… wanted to get that out of my system. Good luck to everyone else in the contest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am very torn; I love Document Lucius especially; but it feels at the same time like a rehash of Samothrace. Can't vote either way.

edit: Actually I am disappointed it doesn't do anything more with the concept than samothrace does already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Without the supplement, the SCP is quite interesting. With it, it becomes horrifying but at the same time nonsensical due to how the lizard people are unchecked and other things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All right… tell me, how does it "fall down" when it comes to the tone and containment procedures? I can get the whole "live in peace" thing is a mistake, and I'm trying to work on how to better handle it, but what's wrong with the containment procedures and tone?

(Also why can I never get this kind of criticism before I fucking post something? -___-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. :( Everything else about the article is great fun, from a solid treatment of one of the oldest alien-themed tropes in the book, to the lies within lies that form the basis of any good conspiracy theory, to the use of crop dusters to delude the masses… Only to completely fall down when it comes to Containment Procedures and, to a lesser extent, clinical tone. Why? ;_; Just so we can have the "they're all around you every day, man" thing going on? There had to be a better way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want to like this - but I can't get over the part where the Foundation decides to just let a breeding population of anomalous humanoids with insider Foundation knowledge "live in peace" because they promise they'll behave.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's funny about this is that, apparently, the Reptilian theory is still -wrong-. They aren't aliens at all. Hell, they aren't even anomalous. Not really. No more than Bigfoot is, anyway. They're earth-life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. Its well written, scientific language, good grammar, and has room for expansion in several ways. Some parts are a bit dense, but that is easily fixed In my opinion. Good Job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Reptilians. Except not really. Except really. I think I would've liked this better if it weren't trying to pretend that it is something other than what it is. But then it would just be an old trope played straight, so I don't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you did a really good job on this. That being said, this is highly dependent on the reveal at the end, which, because this treads plainly in SCP-1000's footsteps, I saw coming a mile away. Now, it's sufficiently different from 1000 that I don't think you should worry about it being a bad article, but for me personally, a lot of the power in what you were trying to do here was sapped by this covering ground that's already been covered.

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
By this point, SCP-2000-B instances

I'm not sure why SCP-2000-B is mentioned before it's explained what it is.

[DATA EXPUNGED]

I don't find this particularly well done.

Overall, nicely done. It's not in my tastes, so no real voting done here (or maybe I'm just tired from lack of sleep) but it's pretty decent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kudos for pulling off the concept so well, but I'm going with a no-vote. This just doesn't mesh with my headcanon well enough for me to truly support it as a potential filler of the 2000 slot. And I must agree with barbequedsteak. It's certainly sci-fi, but more conspiracy sci-fi rather than straight-up, in my opinion at least, and I'm really looking for the latter in what I consider good SCP-2000 candidates. I did enjoy this, however, and I might end up upvoting it if I warm up to the idea more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting. Certainly I can't take this as canon WRT the rest of the Foundation, but I really enjoy it.

E: Wait… no… wait… sorry, I've forgotten how to understand this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I cracked up at the first sentence. Let's hope the rest of it goes over this well.

E: Your collapsible is broken. You might wanna fix that, pronto!

E: Hahaha. Man. Oh, man. Oh, man. That was a wild ride from start to finish. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. Its just the Reptilian conspiracy isn't that much more sci-fi than the rest of the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 1000 made Bigfoot into a believable SCP. This potential 2000 makes the Reptiloid conspiracy into one. There's something about this that pleases me greatly. Take my +1, good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did you read the supplement?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this. However, I don't really see how it fits the theme.

No vote for now
